nodes.label: Brand Strategy First Principle
nodes.contributor: brandkit: rationale that visual system must be based on meaning — category, audience, emotional promise, cultural position, symbolic metaphor all must be inferred before generating
nodes.rationale: Visual systems must be grounded in brand meaning: category, audience, emotional promise, cultural position, and symbolic metaphor must be inferred before any image is generated, to ensure premium and ownable identity.

nodes.label: Logo Generation Standard
nodes.rationale: Logo must be simple, memorable, symbolic, scalable, ownable, visually balanced. Five logo concept methods: Monogram+Meaning, Product Action, Metaphor Fusion, Negative Space, Construction Geometry. Only 1-2 methods combined maximum.

nodes.label: OKLCH Color Space for Canvas Palettes
nodes.norm_label: oklch color space for canvas palettes

nodes.label: CSS Variable Theme System
nodes.rationale: Uses HSL values without the hsl() wrapper to allow opacity modifiers like hsl(var(--primary) / 0.5), enabling runtime theme switching without JavaScript.

nodes.label: Tailwind @theme Directive
nodes.rationale: CSS-based approach to Tailwind customization preferred over JS config for simpler customizations; supports oklch colors, custom breakpoints, and design tokens.

nodes.label: Three Design Dials (v1): Variance, Motion, Density
nodes.rationale: Baseline 8/6/4 for DESIGN_VARIANCE/MOTION_INTENSITY/VISUAL_DENSITY drives all layout, motion, and density decisions without asking users to edit the config file.
nodes.norm_label: three design dials (v1): variance, motion, density

nodes.label: Brief Inference - Read the Room
nodes.rationale: Most LLM design output is bad because the model jumps to a default aesthetic instead of reading the room - inferring page kind, vibe words, reference signals, audience, and existing brand assets first.

nodes.label: Python-Driven True Randomization for Layout
nodes.rationale: LLMs always pick the first layout option due to statistical bias; simulating Python random.choice() with a deterministic seed breaks the loop and ensures unique output selection.

nodes.label: Advanced GSAP Motion and Hover Physics
nodes.norm_label: advanced gsap motion and hover physics

nodes.label: Double-Bezel Nested Architecture Pattern
nodes.rationale: Premium cards must look like physical machined hardware (glass plate in aluminum tray) using outer shell with hairline border and inner core with highlight shadow, creating depth and material presence.

nodes.label: Creative Variance Engine - Vibe and Layout Archetypes
nodes.norm_label: creative variance engine - vibe and layout archetypes

nodes.label: Fluid Island Nav and Hamburger Reveal
nodes.norm_label: fluid island nav and hamburger reveal

nodes.label: Image-First Website Design Workflow
nodes.rationale: Image generation precedes coding so the generated image serves as a design specification and source of truth, preventing generic coded reinterpretations. This enforces: generate first, analyze second, implement third.

nodes.label: Combinatorial Variation Engine
nodes.rationale: Forces non-repetitive AI output by selecting from theme paradigms, hero architectures, section systems, signature components, and motion cues — committing to a coherent visual direction rather than mixing defaults.

nodes.label: Anti-Drift Implementation Rule
nodes.rationale: Prevents the common failure where generated images look strong but the coded result becomes generic. Implementation must remain visually faithful to the image references.

nodes.label: App Design Bible Rule
nodes.rationale: Locking an internal design bible before multi-screen generation ensures consistency across platform mode, device frame, palette, typography, spacing, icon style, and navigation model. Prevents screen 3-5 drifting into a different app identity.

nodes.label: Mobile Image-Only Output Constraint
nodes.rationale: This skill generates mobile screen images only — no code, no SwiftUI, no React Native, no Flutter or HTML. Separates design generation from implementation.

nodes.label: One Horizontal Image Per Section Rule
nodes.rationale: Generates one separate horizontal image per section to prevent text from being too small to read and preserve layout proportions. A landing page with 8 sections produces 8 images. Never compress multiple sections into one image.
nodes.norm_label: one horizontal image per section rule

nodes.label: Narrative Concept Spine
nodes.rationale: Threading one concept metaphor (artifact, journey, tool, living system, stage, archive) across visuals and copy creates a premium scrollscape rather than uniform slab repetition.

nodes.label: Swiss Industrial Print Archetype
nodes.rationale: High-contrast light mode derived from 1960s corporate identity and heavy machinery blueprints. Uses newsprint substrates, monolithic sans-serif, unforgiving structural grids with visible lines, and primary red as the sole accent.

nodes.label: Tactical Telemetry CRT Terminal Archetype
nodes.rationale: Dark-mode-exclusive design derived from military databases and aerospace HUDs. Uses monospaced typography, ASCII framing, phosphor glow, CRT scanlines, and low bit-depth rendering effects.

nodes.label: Blueprint Grid Layout System
nodes.rationale: Strict CSS Grid with gap:1px and contrasting parent/child backgrounds creates mathematically perfect dividing lines. Rejects border-radius entirely — all corners must be exactly 90 degrees for mechanical rigidity.

nodes.label: Warm Monochrome Palette System
nodes.rationale: Uses canvas white (#FFFFFF or #F7F6F3), off-black charcoal (#111111), and exclusively desaturated washed-out pastels as accents. Color is a scarce resource used only for semantic meaning. Gradients, neon, and 3D glassmorphism are banned.

nodes.label: Bento Box Feature Grid Component
nodes.rationale: Asymmetrical CSS Grid layouts with 1px solid #EAEAEA borders, 8-12px max border-radius, and generous 24-40px internal padding. Cards exist only when elevation communicates hierarchy.

nodes.label: Inline Image Typography Technique
nodes.rationale: Embed small contextual photos directly between words or letters in the headline at type-height. Images act as visual punctuation between words. This is the signature creative hero technique for Stitch-generated screens.

nodes.label: Stitch Canvas White + Charcoal Ink Palette
nodes.rationale: Canvas White (#F9FAFB), Pure Surface (#FFFFFF), Charcoal Ink (#18181B), Steel Secondary (#71717A), Muted Slate (#94A3B8), Whisper Border (rgba 226,232,240,0.5). Maximum one accent. Inter font banned. Pure black #000000 banned.
nodes.norm_label: stitch canvas white + charcoal ink palette

nodes.label: Google Stitch DESIGN.md Generation
nodes.rationale: Generates DESIGN.md files as the single source of truth for prompting Google Stitch to produce premium non-generic interfaces. Encodes visual atmosphere, color calibration, typography, component behaviors, layout principles, motion philosophy, and anti-patterns.

nodes.label: 10 UI/UX Rule Categories by Priority
nodes.rationale: Priority order: 1=Accessibility(CRITICAL), 2=Touch&Interaction(CRITICAL), 3=Performance(HIGH), 4=Style(HIGH), 5=Layout&Responsive(HIGH), 6=Typography&Color(MEDIUM), 7=Animation(MEDIUM), 8=Forms&Feedback(MEDIUM), 9=Navigation(HIGH), 10=Charts&Data(LOW).

nodes.label: Hierarchical Design System Persistence
nodes.rationale: MASTER.md global rules with page-specific overrides in design-system/pages/. Page-specific overrides take precedence over master. Enables hierarchical retrieval across sessions.

nodes.label: ICT Award Logo Base Dark Bg (No Year, No Award Text)
nodes.norm_label: ict award logo base dark bg (no year, no award text)
